preAnalysisSummary.rationale: API authorization is already correct. All UI gaps are additive. No schema or API changes needed. No critical blockers identified.

ambiguities.description: TokenService has no role-awareness. It stores the JWT as an opaque string in session with no IsInRole() method.
ambiguities.businessImpact: Without role detection in TokenService, all UI pages would need to decode the JWT manually, leading to inconsistent and error-prone implementations.
ambiguities.proposedResolution: Store user roles in HTTP session at login time. Add IsInRole(string role) to TokenService. Extended StoreTokens overload accepts a roles string.

ambiguities.description: Should a User navigating directly to /Inventory/Create or /Inventory/Edit/{id} see a 403 page or be redirected to /Inventory/Index?
ambiguities.businessImpact: A hard 403 page may confuse legitimate users. A silent redirect with a banner is better UX.
ambiguities.proposedResolution: Redirect to /Inventory/Index with TempData error message: 'You do not have permission to access that page.'

ambiguities.description: OnPostDeleteAsync in IndexModel has no role check. A User role user could craft a raw HTTP POST to invoke the delete handler.
ambiguities.businessImpact: The API returns 403, so no data is actually deleted. However, the Web layer should not silently attempt the call.
ambiguities.proposedResolution: Add IsInRole('Admin') guard in OnPostDeleteAsync before calling the API.

ambiguities.description: Should role be stored in session as a plain string or comma-separated list for future multi-role support?
ambiguities.businessImpact: Low risk for current single-role system. Multi-role could be needed later.
ambiguities.proposedResolution: Store as comma-separated string. IsInRole checks using Contains with OrdinalIgnoreCase.

impactAnalysis.description: Already requires Admin role on POST, PUT, DELETE. No changes needed.
No IsInRole() or role-storage capability. Must add UserRolesKey session key and IsInRole(string role) method.
Add Item button, Edit button per row, and Delete button per row are unconditionally rendered to all authenticated users.
Must expose IsAdmin bool property. OnPostDeleteAsync must add role guard.
OnGet and OnPost only check IsAuthenticated(). User role users can currently load the form. Must add IsInRole('Admin') redirect.
Same gap as Create. OnGetAsync and OnPostAsync must add IsInRole('Admin') redirect.
Login must extract the user's role from the JWT or API response and store it in session via the updated TokenService.StoreTokens().

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should TokenService decode the JWT at parse time, or should roles be stored in session separately at login time?
clarifyingQuestions.suggestedDefault: Store in session at login — avoids JWT decoding on every page load

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should multi-role support be designed into TokenService now?
clarifyingQuestions.suggestedDefault: No — single active role per user. Use comma-separated string to allow future extension without a breaking change.

clarifyingQuestions.question: What should a User see when redirected away from a forbidden page — a toast, a banner, or nothing?
clarifyingQuestions.suggestedDefault: TempData error banner on /Inventory/Index: 'You do not have permission to access that page.'

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should OnPostDeleteAsync in IndexModel also be role-guarded server-side, not just hidden in the UI?
clarifyingQuestions.suggestedDefault: Yes — add role guard to prevent crafted POST requests from non-Admin users reaching the API.

assumptions.text: JWT tokens issued by the API already contain the user's role as a ClaimTypes.Role claim.
assumptions.impactIfWrong: Role cannot be extracted from the JWT at login time without changes to JwtTokenService.

assumptions.text: Users have exactly one role at a time (either Admin or User).
assumptions.impactIfWrong: Multi-role users could get incorrect visibility if only the first role is checked.

assumptions.text: HTTP Session is available and configured in MyProject.Web. TokenService depends on IHttpContextAccessor and session middleware.
assumptions.impactIfWrong: TokenService role storage would not work.

assumptions.text: AuthApiService parses the JWT login response and has access to the raw token string to extract role claims.
assumptions.impactIfWrong: Role extraction at login time requires either JWT parsing or the API returning roles in the login response body.

assumptions.text: The existing LoginResponse DTO from the API includes roles in the body or they can be read from the JWT payload using JwtSecurityTokenHandler.
assumptions.impactIfWrong: JWT parsing with JwtSecurityTokenHandler will need to be added to the Web project.

userStories.title: Extend TokenService with Role Awareness
userStories.capability: have TokenService store the current user's role in session and expose an IsInRole(string role) method
userStories.benefit: all Razor Pages can perform role-based conditional logic without duplicating JWT-decoding code

acceptanceCriteria.text: Given a successful login, when StoreTokens is called with a roles string, then the roles are persisted in the HTTP session under a UserRoles key.
Given a session with role 'Admin' stored, when IsInRole('Admin') is called, then it returns true.
Given a session with role 'User' stored, when IsInRole('Admin') is called, then it returns false.
Given a session with no roles stored or no active session, when IsInRole is called, then it returns false without throwing.
Given a session cleared via Clear(), when IsInRole is called, then it returns false.

nfr.performance: Role check completes in O(n) where n = number of roles — negligible overhead
nfr.security: Roles stored in server-side HTTP session — not readable by client-side JavaScript
nfr.scalability: Session-based; stateless scale-out requires session affinity or distributed session (existing concern)

userStories.title: Hide Admin-Only Controls from User Role on Inventory Index Page
userStories.capability: not see the Add Item button, Edit button, or Delete button on the Inventory list page
userStories.benefit: the UI correctly reflects my permissions and I am not presented with controls that would result in an error if used

acceptanceCriteria.text: Given a logged-in User role user, when they navigate to /Inventory/Index, then the 'Add Item' button is not present in the HTML.
Given a logged-in User role user, when they navigate to /Inventory/Index, then no Edit button is rendered in any table row.
Given a logged-in User role user, when they navigate to /Inventory/Index, then no Delete button is rendered in any table row and the delete confirmation modal is not present.
Given a logged-in Admin role user, when they navigate to /Inventory/Index, then the 'Add Item' button, Edit buttons, and Delete buttons are all rendered.
Given a logged-in User role user, when they submit a crafted form POST to OnPostDeleteAsync, then the handler returns a redirect to /Inventory/Index without calling the API.
Given a logged-in User role user, when they view /Inventory/Index, then the read-only inventory table with all item data (SKU, name, category, stock, price) is still fully visible.

nfr.performance: Conditional rendering is server-side Razor — no additional HTTP calls
nfr.security: Controls hidden using server-side role check, not client-side JavaScript — cannot be bypassed by DOM manipulation

userStories.title: Guard Inventory Create Page from User Role Direct Access
userStories.capability: be redirected away from /Inventory/Create if I navigate to it directly
userStories.benefit: I am never shown a form that I am not permitted to submit, which avoids a confusing 403 error experience

acceptanceCriteria.text: Given a logged-in User role user, when they navigate directly to /Inventory/Create, then they are redirected to /Inventory/Index.
Given the redirect in AC-001, then a TempData error message 'You do not have permission to access that page.' is displayed on the Index page.
Given a logged-in User role user, when they submit a crafted POST to /Inventory/Create, then they are redirected to /Inventory/Index without any item being created.
Given a logged-in Admin role user, when they navigate to /Inventory/Create, then the page loads normally and the form is displayed.
Given an unauthenticated user, when they navigate to /Inventory/Create, then they are redirected to /Account/Login (existing behaviour preserved).

nfr.performance: Role check adds no measurable latency — session read only
nfr.security: Server-side guard prevents the form from rendering — cannot be bypassed with JavaScript or direct URL entry

userStories.title: Guard Inventory Edit Page from User Role Direct Access
userStories.capability: be redirected away from /Inventory/Edit/{id} if I navigate to it directly
userStories.benefit: I am never shown an edit form I am not permitted to submit, which avoids a confusing 403 error experience

acceptanceCriteria.text: Given a logged-in User role user, when they navigate directly to /Inventory/Edit/{valid-guid}, then they are redirected to /Inventory/Index.
Given the redirect in AC-001, then a TempData error message 'You do not have permission to access that page.' is displayed on the Index page.
Given a logged-in User role user, when they submit a crafted POST to /Inventory/Edit/{valid-guid}, then they are redirected to /Inventory/Index without any item being updated.
Given a logged-in Admin role user, when they navigate to /Inventory/Edit/{valid-guid}, then the page loads normally and the pre-populated edit form is displayed.
Given an unauthenticated user, when they navigate to /Inventory/Edit/{valid-guid}, then they are redirected to /Account/Login (existing behaviour preserved).

nfr.performance: Role check adds no measurable latency — session read only
nfr.security: Server-side guard prevents the form from rendering — cannot be bypassed with JavaScript or direct URL entry
